value:
v1.13.0 (2026-04-23) hit a one-time transient "Error creating policy" from GitHub's storage API on softprops/action-gh-release@v3.0.0, leaving the release in DRAFT (3 of 4 artifacts uploaded). Manual recovery published the draft + Homebrew tap. Pinned back to v2.6.2 in #95.

Diagnostic rerun under #96 (2026-04-28) on a debug tag with ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG/RUNNER_DEBUG enabled could NOT reproduce — all 4 artifacts uploaded cleanly in 5-10s on @v3.0.0. Hypothesized cause was the only logic change v2.6.2→v3.0.0: src/github.ts:367 `readableWebStream({type:'bytes'})` → `readableWebStream()`. But the failure looks transient (GitHub API hiccup) rather than deterministic.

PR #98 re-pinned to @v3.0.0 with `continue-on-error: true` attempt 1 + `if: steps.release.outcome == 'failure'` retry. v3 defaults overwrite_files: true so the retry idempotently updates partial releases (no need to set it explicitly).

If v1.14.0+ fails the same way: (1) check if retry caught it (clean run = retry skipped, transient = retry succeeded). (2) If retry also fails, the bug has a real determinant (size? MIME? parallelism count?) worth a deep dive — re-enable debug secrets if removed, push throwaway debug tag, capture full API response body. Don't file upstream without a deterministic repro.
